english: kyi lóg-pa* (kyi log pa*) C. to read mentally, softly, inaudibly; before many verbs yid (yid) stands almost pleon.: *yid kúl-će* (*yid skul ce*) W. to exhort; yid _khrúl-ba (yid 'khrul ba) Sch. 'mental suffering', perh. better: to be uneasy, troubled, harassed; yid _khrúl-ba (yid 'khrul ba) to be mistaken; yid-čad-pa (yid chad pa) v. yi-_čad-pa; (yi 'chad pa;) yid-čes-pa (yid ches pa) to believe, with the accus. or dat. of the thing which one believes, with the dat. of the person whom one believes, ... par, (par,) that ... (cf. dád-pa (dad pa)); kyód-la čuń žig yid ma čés-pas (khyod la chung zhig yid ma ches pas) Mil. having become a little distrustful towards you; *yid (or dén)-čé-čei spe-ra* (*yid (or den) che che'i spe ra*) W. credible words; yid-brtan-dká-ba (yid brtan dka' ba) Tar. not to be depended upon, hardly to be believed; yid-gnyis (yid gnyis) doubt; té-tsom dań yid-gnyis ma byed čig (the tsom dang yid gnyis ma byed cig) Mil.; yid _pám-pa (yid 'pham pa) Mil. to be cast down, dejected, depressed; yid _próg-pa (yid 'phrog pa) Mil. to prepossess, to infatuate; yid bloń-ba (yid blong ba) to be afraid, full of anxiety (?) Sch.; yid _byuń-ba, (yid 'byung ba,) resp. thugs-_byuń-ba (thugs 'byung ba) Mil. to be sad, unhappy, discontented, la, (la,) on account of; ńa kor-bai čos-la yid-byuń-nas (nga 'khor ba'i chos la yid byung nas) Mil. I was wearied of the way of (constantly moving in) the orb of transmigration; yid-_byíń-pa (yid 'bying pa) to make discontented or weary; yid-múg-pa (yid mug pa) v. yi-mug-pa; (yi mug pa;) yid _tsím-pa (yid tshim pa) gyúr-ba (gyur ba) Dzl. to become satisfied, contented; *yid tsim co-ce* (*yid tshim co ce*) W. to satisfy; yid-log-pa (yid log pa) to be tired or weary of Sch.; yid-túń (yid thung) Dzl. forward, rash, overhasty; yid-dúńs (yid dungs) v. duńs; (dungs;) yid-myós (yid myos) fuddled, tipsy; yid-smón (yid smon) v. smón-pa; (smon pa;) yid-gzúńs (yid gzungs) v. gzúńs; (gzungs;) yid - srúbs (yid srubs) Lexx., Sch.: 'a refractory, stubborn mind', which however does not suit the connection. - 2. symb. num.: 14. - II. = yud, (yud,) yid-tsam (yid tsam) for yud-tsam, (yud tsam,) Wdn frq.

resp. and eleg. lágs-pa (lags pa) I. to be, with neg. ma yin (ma yin) or min (min), W. *man* (*man*); kyod su yin (khyod su yin) who are you? gsa de-ka ńa yin (gsa' de ka nga yin) Mil. I was the leopard (you saw); with genit., ńai yin (nga'i yin) that is mine, belongs to me; *di-riń za-nyi-ma yin* (*di ring gza' nyi ma yin*) W. to-day is Sunday; gáń-nas yin (gang nas yin) Mil. whence are you? _di med-pas yin ('di med pas yin) Pth. it is because this is not here ...; ńa bu-moi dús-na yin-te (nga bu mo'i dus na yin te) Glr. when I was still a girl; *yín kyań* (*yin kyang*) C., *yín-na yań* (*yin na yang*) W., C. for _on kyań ('on kyang) yet, nevertheless, notwithstanding; yín-grań(-na) (yin grang (-na)) v. gráń-ba (grang ba) extr.; yin (yin) for optat or imp.: de yin (de yin) 1. so it is, yes. 2. that may be, mi _dod ruń de yin (mi 'dod rung de yin) Mil. if you feel no inclination, never mind, let it be so! dgrá-bo yín-na-_ań yin (dgra bo yin na 'ang yin) Mil. if he is an enemy, let him be so! yín-na (yin na) stands also pleon. with adverbs etc.: sńar yín-na = sńar (sngar yin na = sngar) Mil.; yin, (yin,) so it is! yes! min, (min,) W. *man*, (*man*,) no! yín-mín (yin min) truth in a relative sense, yín-mín-gyi té-tsom bsal (yin min gyi the tsom bsal) Glr. it removes all doubts as to the truth, e.g. the historical truth; ma yín-pa, (ma yin pa,) mín-pa (min pa) 1. vb. not to be a thing. 2. adj. not being a certain thing, ma yín-par, (ma yin par,) adv.; čos ma yíń-par (chos ma yin par) 'not being law', i.e. contrary to the law of religion, wrong, unjust, = mi rígs-par; (mi rigs par;) yul, dus, tsod, rígs-pa ma yín-par spyód-pa (yul, dus, tsod, rigs pa ma yin par spyod pa) Thgy. to do a thing at a wrong time or place, without observing due measure, in an improper or unnatural manner; hence also ma-yín-pa (ma yin pa) alone: wrong, unjust; *ma-yím-pe čó'-pa jhé'-keṇ-la ṭeṇ-šig zer* (*ma yim pe co' pa jhe' keṇ la ṭeṇ shig zer*) C. whoever commits an improper action is called *ṭeṇ-šig*; (*ṭeṇ shig*;) hence also yín-mín (yin min) right and wrong. - 3. v. min. (min.) Cf. moreover yód-pa (yod pa) and _dúg-pa, ('dug pa,) which may be used for yín-pa, (yin pa,) but not inversely. Sometimes it implies to mean, to signify: rńa de či yin (rnga de ci yin) Glr. what does this drumming mean? rgyál-po koń-ráń yin dgońs (rgyal po khong rang yin dgongs) the king thought (the prophecy) meant him, referred to himself; tóg-ma ńéd-kyi pyír ma yín-pas (thog ma nged kyi phyir ma yin pas) Dzl. as from the very beginning it was not aimed at me, had no reference to me; also in other instances, where we have to use words of a more precise character: kyód-kyi lo gań yín-pa-la kó-wo dgú-gis (khyod kyi blo gang yin pa la kho bo dgu gis) Tar. whilst the sensibility that was with you, i.e. the discretion shown by you, gives me much pleasure. - II. yin (yin) is joined to a partic. pres., quite analogous to our English construction: _gró-ba yin ('gro ba yin) I am going Mil., C.; kyód-la lám-mkan yód-pa é yin? (khyod la lam mkhan yod pa e yin?) Mil. (are you having) have you a guide? deí náń-na su yód-pa yin? (de'i nang na su yod pa yin?) Glr. who is within? it is also joined to a partic. pf., when referring to the past: ńa-ráń-la skyés-pa yin (nga rang la skyes pa yin) Glr. I have born him; čád-pa yín-pas (chad pa yin pas) Glr.
